current-outcomes.title: Current outcomes
current-outcomes.prompt: What positive or negative consequences does the current culture actually produce? Write observable results, not aspirations.
examples: Twelve growth workshops last year, but only one idea reached a real customer test
Explore projects must present three-year ROI before budget review, so weak evidence is dressed up as certainty
The core business executes reliably, but the company lacks de-risked future growth options

desired-outcomes.title: Desired outcomes
desired-outcomes.prompt: What should the target innovation culture reliably harvest? The result must be observable or reviewable.
examples: Every quarter, 3–5 critical assumptions are validated or invalidated with real customer evidence
High-potential projects transfer from Explore into business units, while weak ideas are retired cheaply
Explore and Exploit portfolios are visible to leadership and evaluated with different standards

current-behaviors.title: Current behaviors
current-behaviors.prompt: What do people repeatedly do or say today? Capture evidence like an anthropologist; avoid abstract judgment.
examples: Teams say 'we need the full plan first' and then spend six weeks without customer contact
Leaders ask for revenue forecasts in reviews, but rarely ask what was learned last week
After failed projects, the team reviews execution against plan, not which assumptions were wrong

desired-behaviors.title: Desired behaviors
desired-behaviors.prompt: Which concrete behaviors must become normal and rewarded to produce the desired outcomes?
examples: Every Explore project names its three most critical and riskiest assumptions first
Teams earn larger resources by producing evidence through small tests
Reviews ask for evidence, learning velocity, and risk reduction before scale plans

current-enablers-blockers.title: Current enablers / blockers
current-enablers-blockers.prompt: Which formal or informal mechanisms make current behaviors happen? Which mechanisms prevent useful behaviors?
examples: Enabler: an executive protects small experiment budgets; blocker: all projects use mature-business KPIs
Enabler: monthly product retrospectives; blocker: reviews discuss schedule, not falsified assumptions
Blocker: business units own resource vetoes, so Explore projects cannot find transfer owners

desired-enablers-blockers.title: Enablers to build / blockers to remove
desired-enablers-blockers.prompt: What enablers must be built, and which blockers must be removed or redesigned to cultivate the target culture?
examples: Build: separate Explore metrics, evidence review cadence, and a small experiment fund
Build: a Transfer gate that defines when a business unit accepts an Explore project
Remove: full business plans before small tests; redesign: failed tests do not hurt promotion, but hiding evidence does
